steps.title: Restate the conditions
steps.content: An ordered pair $(x, y)$ is a solution when BOTH $y \leq x$ and $y \leq -x$ hold. Since the choices are simple integer pairs, plug each one into both inequalities.

steps.title: Test every choice
steps.content:
• A. $(1, 0)$: $0 \leq 1$ ✓ but $0 \leq -1$ ✗; fails.
  • B. $(-1, 0)$: $0 \leq -1$ ✗; fails.
  • C. $(0, 1)$: $1 \leq 0$ ✗; fails.
  • D. $(0, -1)$: $-1 \leq 0$ ✓ and $-1 \leq 0$ ✓; satisfies both.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
